guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Use only commands available in the selected public release.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: The v0.5.3 black-box journey creates both adoption and configuration Work Items using public commands.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Every configuration edit must be owned by the second adoption Work Item.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: Docs and journey tests create configure_ai_cockpit before Doctor, Calibration, Profile, Guard, quality, and CI edits.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Generated status must be reproducible from current repository state.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: No-active consistency compares listed paths with live Git changes while excluding current_status.md itself.

risk.detail: Existing consumers pinned to v0.5.2 remain reproducible; the public pointer and default now select v0.5.3.

observedIssues: Strict live status validation initially exposed stale installer-generated status after the first commit; ai-start now repairs only that generated-state mismatch.

residualRisks.detail: Users must still review and tailor the configure_ai_cockpit Contract scope before configuration edits.
Preset-only stacks remain configurable rather than hosted-verified.

reviewReadiness.reason: Release pointer, two-stage governance, live status consistency, and stack selection are implemented and verified.

userCorrectionsCaptured: v0.5.3 was public while release pointers remained v0.5.2; calibration lacked a second Work Item; status could be stale.
userCorrectionSolidification: Three README flows require configure_ai_cockpit before Doctor.
Status consistency validates live Git paths.

checkpointEvidence.detail: Reviewed v0.5.3 distribution, two-stage ownership, status consistency, and full quality evidence.

knownGaps: A future release may add a dedicated configuration preset to reduce manual Contract tailoring.
overclaimPrevention: Documentation continues to distinguish configurable presets from hosted verification and requires explicit project adaptation.
